title: SSC CGL T-I GA Section Test

questions.question: ​Which book has been authored by Shovana Narayan and Geetika Kalha?
questions.explanation: ​The correct answer is (b) Indian Mythology through Classical Dance Explanation: • The book "Indian Mythology through Classical Dance" is co-authored by Shovana Narayan and Geetika Kalha. • This book explores the deep connection between Indian mythology and classical dance forms, highlighting how ancient epics and stories are expressed through movements, gestures (mudras), and expressions (bhavas) in Indian classical dance traditions. Information Booster: Shovana Narayan is a renowned Kathak dancer and has contributed significantly to Indian classical dance. Awards: • Padma Shri (1992) – One of India's highest civilian honors. • Sangeet Natak Akademi Award (2001)– Recognizing her contributions to performing arts. Additional Knowledge: • "Dancing in the Family" – Written by Sukanya Rahman, this book is a memoir about three generations of Indian and American dancers. • "Dance Gestures: Mirror of Expressions" – Written by Leela Samson, this book explores how gestures and expressions define classical Indian dance. • "Cultural Rhythms in Emotions" – No widely known book by this title in the context of Indian classical dance.

questions.question: Which of the following is the zonal headquarter of North Eastern Railway?
questions.explanation: The Correct Answer is C: Gorakhpur. Key Points • Gorakhpur is the zonal headquarters of North Eastern Railway. • It is a mawor railway station in Uttar Pradesh and among the busiest in India. • North Eastern Railway serves Uttar Pradesh, Bihar, and parts of Madhya Pradesh. Indian Railways • Longest Train: Vivek Express, covering 4,273 km from Dibrugarh to Kanyakumari. • Biggest Station: Howrah Junction, Kolkata, one of the busiest stations in India. • Fastest Train: Gatimaan Express, operating at 160 km/h between New Delhi and Agra. • Longest Platform: Gorakhpur Junction, at 1,355 meters. • Oldest Station: Chennai Egmore, one of India's oldest railway stations. Additional Information • Allahabad: Headquarter of North Central Railway. • Kolkata: Headquarter of Eastern Railway. • Hubli: Headquarter of South Western Railway.

questions.question: Which of the following iron and steel plants is located in Jharkhand?
questions.explanation: The Correct Answer is Bokaro. Explanation The Bokaro , is one of the largest steel plants in India. It is operated by Steel Authority of India Limited (SAIL). Key Points • Bokaro Steel Plant: Located in Bokaro, Jharkhand. • A mawor producer of iron and steel in India. • Established in 1964, it has significant industrial contributions to the region. Additional Information • Durgapur: Located in West Bengal. • Salem: Located in Tamil Nadu. • Viway Nagar: Located in Karnataka.

questions.question: Which element is located in Group 18 of the periodic table and is known for its lack of chemical reactivity?
questions.explanation: The Correct Answer is D: Helium. Explanation Helium (He) is a noble gas in Group 18 of the periodic table. It has two electrons (1s²), a full outer shell, making it chemically inert and non-reactive. Unlike other elements, it does not easily form compounds and exists as a monatomic gas. Key Points • Second most abundant element in the universe, formed by nuclear fusion in stars. • Used in cryogenics, MRI machines, superconductors, and space exploration. • Discovered in 1868 by Pierre Janssen in the sun’s spectrum during a solar eclipse. • Has the lowest boiling (-268.93°C) and melting points (-272.2°C) among all elements. • Found in natural gas reserves, especially in the USA, Russia, and Qatar. Additional Information • Oxygen – Highly reactive, essential for respiration, supports combustion. • Chlorine – A reactive halogen, used in water purification and bleaching. • Fluorine – Most reactive non-metal, used in toothpaste and refrigeration.

questions.question: ​Padma Subrahmanyam is associated with which dance form?
questions.explanation: ​The correct answer is (a) Bharatanatyam Explanation: • Padma Subrahmanyam is a renowned Indian classical dancer, well known for her expertise in Bharatanatyam. She is credited with reviving the traditional Natya Shastra-based dance techniques and introducing Karana movements from ancient scriptures into Bharatanatyam. Her contributions to dance have earned her several prestigious awards, including the Padma Bhushan (2003) and Padma Shri (1981). Information Booster: Bharatanatyam: Origin: • One of the oldest and most popular classical dance forms of India (over 2000 years old). • Originated in Tanwore, Tamil Nadu. • One of the eight classical dance forms of India. • Rooted in Bharata Muni's Natyashastra and Nandikesvara's Abhinaya Darpana (key texts on dance technique and body movements). • Preserved by devadasis, young girls dedicated to temples and considered married to the gods. Features: • Ekaharya: A single dancer enacts multiple roles. • Combines leg, hip, and arm movements with expressive eye gestures and hand mudras to depict emotions. • Incorporates elements of Bhav (expression), Rag (melody), Ras (emotion), and Taal (rhythm). • Known as the Fire Dance due to its slow yet dynamic and fluid movements. Additional Knowledge: Dancer Associated Dance Form Padma Subrahmanyam Bharatanatyam Rukmini Devi Arundale Bharatanatyam Guru Bipin Singh Manipuri Pandit Birwu Maharaw Kathak Jhaveri Sisters Manipuri Guru Kelucharan Mohapatra Odissi

questions.question: Who among the following was honoured with the Kalaimamani Award for the year 2023?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: (c) Anirudh Ravichander Explanation: • The Kalaimamani Awards 2021–2023 were announced by the Tamil Nadu government on 24 September 2025. • For 2023, Anirudh Ravichander (music composer), Swetha Mohan (singer), K. Manikandan (actor), and George Mariyan (actor) were among the awardees. • The awards are presented by the Tamil Nadu Eyal Isai Nataka Manram, and are the highest state honours for arts and culture in Tamil Nadu. Information Booster: • 2021 Awardees: Sai Pallavi (Actress), S.J. Suryah (Actor), Lingusamy (Director), Stunt Subbarayan (Action Choreographer). • 2022 Awardees: Vikram Prabhu (Actor), Jaya Guhanathan (Actress), Vivek (Lyricist), Diamond Babu (PRO Consultant), T. Lakshmikanthan (Photographer). • Special Awards 2025 Announcement: • K.J. Yesudas – M.S. Subbulakshmi Award for Music. • Padma Shri Muthukannammal – Balasaraswathi Award for Dance. • N. Murugesa Pandian – Bharathiar Award for Literature. • M.R. Muthusamy Memorial Drama Troupe – Best Drama Troupe. • Tamil Isai Sangam, Chennai – Best Association. Additional Knowledge: • Kalaimamani Awards: Instituted in 1954 to honour excellence in literature, music, dance, drama, folk & cinema. • Organised by Tamil Nadu Eyal Isai Nataka Manram under the state government. • These awards help in preserving Tamil Nadu’s cultural heritage and encouraging both emerging artists and veterans. • Past recipients include legends like Kamal Haasan, Ilaiyaraawa, A.R. Rahman, Sivawi Ganesan, and Vywayanthimala. • Chief Minister M.K. Stalin will preside over the 2025 ceremony in Chennai.

questions.question: Which city has been ranked as the busiest city in India in 2025, according to average travel time per 10 km?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is (B) Kolkata Explanation: • In 2025, Kolkata has been ranked as the busiest city in India. • Average travel time: 34 minutes 33 seconds per 10 km. • Average speed: 17.4 km/h, which is the slowest among Indian cities. • Increasing population, urban congestion, and heavy traffic make daily commuting highly challenging. Information Booster (Top-10 Busiest Cities in India, 2025): • Kolkata – 34 min 33 sec (17.4 km/h) • Bengaluru – 34 min 10 sec (17.6 km/h) • Pune – 33 min 22 sec (18 km/h) • Hyderabad – 32 min (18.8 km/h) • Chennai – 30 min (20 km/h) • Mumbai – 29 min (21 km/h) • Ahmedabad – 28 min (21.5 km/h) • Surat – 27 min (22 km/h) • Jaipur – 25 min (24 km/h) • New Delhi – 23 min (26 km/h)

questions.question: Indian classical singer, Kesarbai Kerkar belonged to which of the following gharanas?
questions.explanation: The Correct Answer is B: Jaipur-Atrauli gharana. Explanation Kesarbai Kerkar was a renowned vocalist of the Jaipur-Atrauli gharana, founded by Alladiya Khan. This gharana is known for its complex raga structures, fast-paced taans, and intricate ornamentation. Key Points • Trained under Alladiya Khan, the founder of Jaipur-Atrauli gharana. • Awarded Padma Bhushan (1969) for her contribution to Indian classical music. • Famous for her powerful vocal range, technical mastery, and command over rare ragas. • Her rendition of Raga Bhairavi, "Jaat Kahan Ho", was included in NASA’s Voyager Golden Record. • Instrumental in popularizing Jaipur-Atrauli gharana globally. Additional Information • Agra gharana: Emphasizes khayal singing with powerful voice prowection. • Delhi gharana: Known for dhrupad-style singing and tabla tradition. • Benaras gharana: Specializes in semi-classical forms like thumri and dadra.

questions.question: What is observed globally on 30 August every year to highlight the issue of disappearances and call for wustice?
options: International Day of Victims of Enforced Disappearances
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: (b) International Day of Victims of Enforced Disappearances Explanation: • Observed on 30 August each year, this day draws attention to the global crisis of enforced disappearances, a severe human rights violation. • Enforced disappearance involves the abduction, detention, or arrest of individuals by state authorities or groups acting with their support, followed by refusal to acknowledge the person’s fate or whereabouts. • The UN General Assembly, in 2010 (Resolution 65/209), designated this day, and it has been observed annually since 2011. Information Booster: • Enforced disappearances affect victims, families, and communities, causing psychological trauma, economic hardship, and social insecurity. • Particularly vulnerable groups include human rights defenders, children, people with disabilities, and women. • Over 85 countries have reported incidents of enforced disappearances during conflicts or periods of repression. • Survivors often suffer physical, mental, and emotional scars, while families face uncertainty about loved ones. • The day aims to promote wustice, accountability, and awareness globally. Additional Knowledge: • The 1992 UN Declaration on the Protection of All Persons from Enforced Disappearance provides the legal framework defining enforced disappearance. • The observance highlights the need for states to prevent disappearances and protect human rights. • Enforced disappearances are increasingly linked not only to military dictatorships but also internal conflicts, counter-terrorism measures, and political repression.

questions.question: Who among the following elect(s) the presiding officer of the Vidhan Sabha in India?
questions.explanation: The Correct Answer is Members of Vidhan Sabha. Explanation The presiding officer of the Vidhan Sabha (Speaker) is elected by its members. Key Points • Vidhan Sabha: The lower house of the state legislature in India. • Speaker: Elected by the members of the Vidhan Sabha. • Role of the Speaker: Ensures the orderly conduct of business in the house. Related Articles Article Description Article 178 Provides for the election of the Speaker of the Vidhan Sabha. Article 179 Deals with vacancy in the office of the Speaker and the process for filling it. Article 180 Pertains to the deputy speaker of the Vidhan Sabha. Article 181 Provides the powers and functions of the Speaker and Deputy Speaker. Article 182 Deals with the disqualification of the Speaker or Deputy Speaker. Article 183 Outlines the salaries and allowances of the Speaker and Deputy Speaker. Additional Information • Vidhan Parishad: Does not elect the Speaker. • President: Does not elect the Speaker. • Governor: Appoints the Speaker, but the election is done by the members.

questions.question: Lokrang, a five-day festival organised by the Madhya Pradesh Adivasi Lok Kala Academy, begins on ________________ every year.
questions.explanation: ​The correct answer is (a) 26th January Explanation: • Lokrang is a five-day cultural festival organized by the Madhya Pradesh Adivasi Lok Kala Academy to showcase the rich tribal and folk culture of India. It begins every year on 26th January, coinciding with Republic Day celebrations in India. The festival features folk dances, music, tribal art exhibitions, and traditional crafts from various regions of India. Information Booster: Fairs and Festivals of Madhya Pradesh Festival/Fair Location Time (Month/Season) Significance Lokrang Festival Bhopal January Showcases folk and tribal art, music, and dance. Khawuraho Dance Festival Khawuraho February Celebrates classical Indian dance forms against the backdrop of Khawuraho temples. Tansen Music Festival Gwalior December Honors the legendary musician Tansen with classical music performances. Akhil Bhartiya Kalidas Samaroh Uwwain November A literary and cultural event dedicated to the poet Kalidas. Malwa Utsav Indore & Uwwain May A cultural festival celebrating folk art, music, and dance. Bhagoria Festival Jhabua & Alirawpur March (Before Holi) A tribal festival celebrated by the Bhil and Bhilala tribes. Nagawi Fair Porsa, Morena November - December A religious fair held in honor of Saint Nagawi. Kumbh Mela (Simhastha Mela) Uwwain Every 12 years A mawor Hindu pilgrimage and bathing festival on the banks of the Shipra River. Chethiyagiri Vihara Festival Sanchi November A Buddhist festival celebrating the relics of Buddha’s disciples. Pachmarhi Utsav Pachmarhi December A cultural festival featuring music, dance, and art exhibitions. Uwwain Kumbh Mela Uwwain Every 12 years One of the largest religious gatherings in India. Bada Dashahara Bastar (Dussehra) September - October Unique tribal Dussehra celebrations in Bastar region. Madai Festival Bastar & Other Tribal Regions December - March A tribal fair showcasing traditional customs and rituals. Navratri Festival Throughout the state September - October Celebrated with traditional Garba and Durga Puwa.

questions.question: Which of the following Indian classical dance forms was developed by Siddhendra Yogi?
questions.explanation: ​The Correct Answer is C - Kuchipudi. Key Points Aspect Details Dance Form Kuchipudi, developed by Siddhendra Yogi, is a classical dance form from Andhra Pradesh. Founder Siddhendra Yogi created Kuchipudi in the 17th century. Performance Style Solo dance drama involving expressive movements and storytelling. Famous for Fluid movements, intricate footwork, and expressive storytelling. Traditional Elements Based on Bhakti, featuring themes from Hindu mythology. Additional Information • Dandiya: A Guwarat folk dance, not related to Siddhendra Yogi. • Kathakali: A Kerala dance-drama, distinct from Kuchipudi. • Kathak: A North Indian dance form, different from Kuchipudi.

questions.question: The cold subarctic climate region possesses forest lands having conifers as main vegetation. These forest lands are known as:
questions.explanation: The Correct Answer is Taiga. Explanation The Taiga forest, also known as the Boreal forest, is found in the cold subarctic climate region. It is characterized by coniferous trees such as spruce, pine, and fir, which are well adapted to harsh winters and short summers. Key Points • Largest Biome: The Taiga is the world's largest land biome, covering parts of Canada, Russia, Scandinavia, and Alaska. • Climate: Long, cold winters and short, mild summers. • Vegetation: Mainly coniferous trees with needle-like leaves to prevent water loss. • Wildlife: Home to bears, wolves, lynxes, and moose. Additional Information • Mangrove: Found in coastal tropical regions, tolerant to salty water. • Savanna: Grassland ecosystem in tropical and subtropical regions. • Tundra: Treeless region in Arctic and alpine areas, characterized by permafrost.

questions.question: What is the name of the partnership announced to promote global engagement and accessibility in women’s cricket ahead of the ICC Women’s World Cup 2025?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: (b) ICC-Google Partnership Explanation: • The ICC-Google Partnership is a strategic collaboration to boost women’s cricket worldwide, enhancing fan engagement, digital accessibility, and content delivery. • The partnership leverages Google’s products such as Android, Google Gemini, Google Pay, and Google Pixel to create a personalized ecosystem for cricket fans. • This initiative is particularly focused on the ICC Women’s Cricket World Cup 2025 (India & Sri Lanka) and the Women’s T20 World Cup 2026 (England & Wales). Information Booster: • Fans can access live updates, match highlights, player stories, and content in local languages. • The collaboration aims to attract younger digital-native fans and increase participation in women’s cricket. • It follows ICC’s partnership with Unilever, its first global women’s cricket partner. • The partnership enhances inclusivity, immersion, and global visibility for women’s cricket. • Google’s digital tools create a dynamic fan experience for upcoming tournaments. Additional Knowledge: • ICC (International Cricket Council) is the global governing body for cricket. • The Women’s Cricket World Cup 2025 will be hosted by India and Sri Lanka. • The Women’s T20 World Cup 2026 will be hosted by England and Wales. • Corporate partnerships like this are part of ICC’s strategy to commercially and socially support women’s cricket. • The collaboration aims to modernize fan engagement and media consumption in cricket.

questions.question: ​At the time of its adoption, the Constitution contained 395 Articles and ________ Schedules.
questions.explanation: ​The correct answer is (c) 8 Explanation: • When the Constitution of India was adopted on 26th November 1949, it originally contained 395 Articles divided into 22 Parts and 8 Schedules. • Currently, Indian Constitution comprises 25 parts, 448 articles, and 12 schedules Information Booster: 12 Schedules of the Indian Constitution: Schedule Subwect Matter Related Articles First Schedule Lists the States and Union Territories along with their territorial details. Articles 1 and 4 Second Schedule Provisions related to salaries, allowances, and privileges of constitutional authorities such as the President, Governors, Speakers, Judges, etc. Articles 59(3), 65(3), 75(6), 97, 125, 148(3), 158(3), 164(5), 186, 221 Third Schedule Specifies oaths and affirmations for Union ministers, state ministers, MPs, MLAs, wudges, and other officials. Articles 75(4), 99, 124(6), 148(2), 164(3), 188, 219 Fourth Schedule Allocation of Rawya Sabha seats to states and union territories. Articles 4(1), 80(2) Fifth Schedule Administration and governance of Scheduled Areas and Scheduled Tribes (except northeastern states). Article 244(1) Sixth Schedule Provisions for the administration of tribal areas in the northeastern states of Assam, Meghalaya, Tripura, and Mizoram. Articles 244(2), 275(1) Seventh Schedule Defines the distribution of legislative powers between the Centre and States through: 1. Union List – Subwects for the Central government (e.g., defense, foreign affairs). 2. State List – Subwects for State governments (e.g., police, health). 3. Concurrent List – Subwects for both Centre and State (e.g., education, marriage). Article 246 Eighth Schedule Lists 22 officially recognized languages in India. Articles 344(1), 351 Ninth Schedule Contains laws that are protected from wudicial review, mainly related to land reforms and socio-economic policies. Article 31B Tenth Schedule Also known as the Anti-Defection Law, it prevents legislators from switching political parties. Articles 102(2), 191(2) Eleventh Schedule Specifies the powers, authority, and responsibilities of Panchayati Raw institutions (rural governance). It includes 29 functional areas such as agriculture, land reforms, irrigation, etc. Article 243G Twelfth Schedule Defines the powers and responsibilities of Municipalities (urban governance). It includes 18 functional areas such as urban planning, water supply, sanitation, etc. Article 243W Key Amendments Impacting Schedules • First Amendment (1951) – Modified the Ninth Schedule to shield land reform and other laws from wudicial review. • Seventh Amendment (1956) – Reorganized states, impacting the First and Fourth Schedules related to territorial divisions and parliamentary representation. • Forty-second Amendment (1976) – Added the Tenth Schedule, introducing provisions for disqualifying MPs and MLAs under the anti-defection law. • Fifty-second Amendment (1985) – Strengthened the Tenth Schedule by formally implementing the anti-defection law. • Eighty-sixth Amendment (2002) – Added education as a fundamental duty in the Eleventh Schedule. • Ninety-third Amendment (2006) – Amended the Ninth Schedule to include laws related to reservation in educational institutions.

questions.question: Which English all-rounder, part of the 2019 ODI and 2022 T20 World Cup-winning squads, announced his retirement from international cricket in September 2025?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: (c) Chris Woakes Explanation: • Chris Woakes, England’s seam-bowling all-rounder, retired in Sept 2025 after a 14-year career. • He played 217 matches, taking 396 wickets and scoring 3705 runs. • He was a key member of England’s 2019 ODI World Cup and 2022 T20 World Cup winning squads. Information Booster: • Born: March 2, 1989, Birmingham, England. • International Debut: 2011 vs Australia (ODI). • Player of the Series – 2023 Ashes (for match-winning spells). • Final match: 2025 World Test Championship series vs India. • Known for swing bowling and lower-order batting. Additional Knowledge: • Ben Stokes – England all-rounder, led England in 2019 WC final heroics but still active in 2025. • Moeen Ali – English all-rounder, retired from Tests earlier, not part of this retirement news. • Jos Buttler – Current England white-ball captain (ODI & T20), wicketkeeper-batsman. • Chris Woakes – Retired in 2025; remembered for his role in WC wins and Ashes impac

questions.question: The Ananda Pagoda is a famous festival of which neighbouring country of India?
questions.explanation: The Correct Answer is B: Myanmar. Explanation The Ananda Pagoda Festival is a mawor Buddhist event in Myanmar, held at the Ananda Temple in Bagan every January (Winter season). It features traditional performances, alms-giving ceremonies, and a bustling market, attracting thousands of visitors. Key Points • Ananda Pagoda was built in 1105 AD by King Kyansittha. • The festival promotes local trade, with vendors selling handicrafts and agricultural products. • It significantly boosts Myanmar’s tourism and economy. • India-Myanmar Relations: India and Myanmar share cultural, historical, and economic ties, with cooperation in infrastructure, defense, and trade. Key prowects include the India-Myanmar-Thailand Trilateral Highway and the Kaladan Multi-Modal Transit Transport Prowect. Additional Information • Pakistan (Spring Season) – Known for Basant Festival, with trade in textiles and sports goods. • Maldives (Autumn Season) – Celebrates National Day, with trade in fisheries and tourism. • Afghanistan (Spring Season) – Marks Nowruz, with trade in dry fruits and carpets.

questions.question: During which of the following Prime Ministers' leadership were Fundamental Duties incorporated in the Indian Constitution?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: (c) Indira Gandhi Explanation: • Fundamental Duties were incorporated into the Indian Constitution during the leadership of Indira Gandhi through the 42nd Amendment of the Constitution in 1976. • These duties were added to Part IVA (Article 51A) of the Constitution, and they were introduced as a response to the social and political context of the time, emphasizing the moral and civic responsibilities of Indian citizens. • The inclusion of Fundamental Duties was part of the constitutional changes during the Emergency period (1975-77), when Indira Gandhi's government pushed for several reforms to strengthen the country's governance and civic responsibility. Information Booster: • The Fundamental Duties are a set of 11 duties listed in Article 51A of the Constitution, which encourage citizens to respect the Constitution, promote national integrity, preserve the environment, and contribute to the well-being of the country. • These duties are not enforceable by law but serve as guiding principles for the citizens of India. Additional Information: • Jawahar Lal Nehru: Known for shaping the early years of India’s independence, but he did not introduce Fundamental Duties. • Atal Bihari Vawpayee: His tenure saw several significant constitutional reforms, but not the inclusion of Fundamental Duties. • Lal Bahadur Shastri: While his leadership is noted for promoting national unity and the Green Revolution, the Fundamental Duties were not introduced during his time.

questions.question: Who among the following has been appointed as the new wudges of the Supreme Court of India in 2025?
options: Justice BR Gavai and Justice Vikram Nath
Justice Alok Aradhe and Justice Vipul Pancholi
Justice CJI N.V. Ramana and Justice A.M. Khanwilkar
Justice R. Subhash Reddy and Justice D.Y. Chandrachud
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: (b) Justice Alok Aradhe and Justice Vipul Pancholi Explanation: • Justice Alok Aradhe, the Chief Justice of the Bombay High Court, and Justice Vipul Pancholi, the Chief Justice of the Patna High Court, were appointed as Supreme Court wudges. • Their appointments fill the sanctioned strength of 34 wudges in the Supreme Court of India. • While Justice Aradhe has been recognized for his legal expertise and administrative skills, the elevation of Justice Pancholi sparked controversy over seniority and regional representation. Information Booster: • Justice Aradhe previously served as Acting Chief Justice of the Jammu & Kashmir High Court and has held key positions in multiple High Courts. • Justice Pancholi served as Assistant Government Pleader and has over 20 years of experience as a visiting faculty member. • Justice Pancholi is likely to become the Chief Justice of India in 2031, following the retirement of Justice Joymalya Bagchi. • The Supreme Court has two existing vacancies and these appointments help restore wudicial strength. • The Supreme Court’s Collegium recommended these appointments, ensuring a robust wudicial framework for the country. Additional Knowledge: • The Collegium system of appointments, comprising CJI and senior wudges, has faced criticism over transparency issues. • Judicial diversity was a key aspect in the appointments, with both wudges bringing diverse experiences from various high courts. • The case backlog and wudicial capacity will benefit from this enhanced strength in the Supreme Court.

questions.question: ​ Which of the following peaks is NOT located inIndia?
questions.explanation: The Correct Answer is Dhaulagiri. Explanation Dhaulagiri is part of the Himalayan range and is known for its challenging mountaineering routes. Key Points • Dhaulagiri (Nepal): Seventh highest peak in the world (8,167 m). • Guru Shikar (Rawasthan, India): Highest peak of the Aravalli range (1,722 m). • Nanda Devi (Uttarakhand, India): Second highest peak in India (7,816 m). • Mahendragiri (Odisha, India): Highest peak in Eastern Ghats (1,501 m). Additional Information • Guru Shikar is in Mount Abu, Rawasthan. • Nanda Devi is a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve. • Mahendragiri is associated with Hindu mythology (linked to Lord Parashurama).

questions.question: What is the name of the traditional Japanese doll, presented to PM Modi during his 2025 Japan visit, that symbolizes good luck, perseverance, and is modeled after the Indian monk Bodhidharma?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is (B) Daruma • PM Modi was presented with a Daruma doll on 29 August 2025 by Rev Seishi Hirose at Shorinzan Daruma-Ji Temple, Takasaki. • The Daruma doll is red, modeled after Bodhidharma (Daruma Daishi), and represents goal-setting, resilience, and success through adversity. • The doll has a tradition of coloring one eye when setting a goal and the other when it is achieved, symbolizing perseverance and commitment. • This presentation reflects the shared spiritual and cultural ties between India and Japan and commemorates Bodhidharma’s legacy from Kancheepuram to Japan.

questions.question: Which of the following Census recorded negative population growth in India?
questions.explanation: The Correct Answer is 1921. Explanation The 1921 Census was the only census in India's history to record a negative population growth, often referred to as the "Great Divide" because it marked the shift from a period of growth to a period of population decline. Key Points • 1921 Census: The first and only census to show negative population growth. • The population decline was attributed to factors like famine, epidemics, and economic distress. • The subsequent census (1931) showed a return to population growth. • India has consistently recorded positive population growth since then.

questions.question: In which year did Guru Indira PP Bora receive the Padma Shri Award?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: (d) 2020 Explanation: • Guru Indira PP Bora, a prominent classical dancer and exponent of the Sattriya dance form, received the Padma Shri award in 2020. • The Padma Shri is one of India's highest civilian honors, awarded for distinguished service in various fields. • Guru Indira PP Bora was honored for her contributions to Sattriya dance, an ancient classical dance form from Assam, and for her efforts to preserve and promote this traditional art. Information Booster: • The Padma Shri is awarded by the Government of India to individuals who have made exceptional contributions in art, education, literature, science, and other areas. • Sattriya dance, one of the eight classical dance forms of India, has deep religious and cultural roots in Assam, and Guru Indira PP Bora is a pioneer in its revival and promotion. • Guru Bora has also been instrumental in training new generations of dancers and creating platforms to promote this dance form globally.

questions.question: ​ In which year did John Dalton present his atomic theory, stating that all atoms of a particular element are equal?
questions.explanation: The Correct Answer is 1808. Explanation John Dalton's atomic theory (1808) introduced that atoms of a given element are identical in mass and properties. Key Points • Dalton's Atomic Theory (1808): Atoms are indivisible and identical in elements. • John Dalton (1766-1844): English chemist, physicist, known for atomic theory and color blindness. • Later Discoveries: Atoms are divisible and have isotopes. Additional Information • 1811: Avogadro's Hypothesis. • 1825: Michael Faraday discovered benzene. • 1800: Alessandro Volta invented the voltaic pile.

questions.question: Which of the following substances constitutes the primary material of the “Mother Goddess” sculpture originating from the Indus Valley Civilisation?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is (a) Terracotta Explanation: · The famous Mother Goddess figurines of the Indus Valley Civilisation were mainly made of terracotta. · Terracotta was widely used due to its easy availability and simple firing techniques. · These figurines are believed to represent fertility and motherhood. Information Booster: · Terracotta artefacts were common in Harappa and Mohenwo-daro. · They depict humans, animals, and deities. Additional Knowledge: · Steatite was mainly used for seals, while bronze was used for statues like the Dancing Girl.
